infobox.value: Ōsaka Hai, [Tennō Shō](/wiki/Tenn%C5%8D_Sh%C5%8D "Tennō Shō") (Spring), [Takarazuka Kinen](/wiki/Takarazuka_Kinen "Takarazuka Kinen")[[1]](#cite_note-1) **Domestic:** ¥ 300,000,000 **International:** ¥ 150,000,000 **Winner of any three of the following in the same year:** Ōsaka Hai, [Tennō Shō](/wiki/Tenn%C5%8D_Sh%C5%8D "Tennō Shō") (Spring/Autumn), [Japan Cup](/wiki/Japan_Cup "Japan Cup"), [Takarazuka Kinen](/wiki/Takarazuka_Kinen "Takarazuka Kinen"), [Arima Kinen](/wiki/Arima_Kinen "Arima Kinen") **Domestic:** ¥ 200,000,000 **International:** ¥ 100,000,000 |

(大阪杯) is a [Grade 1](/wiki/Conditions_races "Conditions races") [horse race](/wiki/Horse_racing "Horse racing") in Japan for [Thoroughbred](/wiki/Thoroughbred "Thoroughbred") [colts](/wiki/Colt_(horse) "Colt (horse)") and [fillies](/wiki/Filly "Filly") aged four and over run over a distance of 2,000 metres at [Hanshin Racecourse](/wiki/Hanshin_Racecourse "Hanshin Racecourse"). The race is also known as the **[Sankei](/wiki/Sankei_Shimbun "Sankei Shimbun") Ōsaka Hai** (産経大阪杯) (until 2016) and the Ōsaka Cup. The race is run in early April.
* Fastest: Bellagio Opera (2025): 1:56.2
* 2 — Sunrise Pegasus: (2002, 2005)
* 6 — Yutaka Take: Mejiro McQueen (1993), Marvelous Sunday (1997), Air Groove (1998), Kizuna (2014), Kitasan Black (2017), Jack d'Or (2023)
* 3 — Yasutoshi Ikee: Dream Journey (2009), Orfevre (2013), Al Ain (2019)
* 4 — Sunday Racing: Dream Journey (2009), Orfevre (2013), Al Ain (2019), Lucky Lilac (2020)

Horse race

The **Ōsaka Hai** (大阪杯) is a [Grade 1](/wiki/Conditions_races "Conditions races") [horse race](/wiki/Horse_racing "Horse racing") in Japan for [Thoroughbred](/wiki/Thoroughbred "Thoroughbred") [colts](/wiki/Colt_(horse) "Colt (horse)") and [fillies](/wiki/Filly "Filly") aged four and over run over a distance of 2,000 metres at [Hanshin Racecourse](/wiki/Hanshin_Racecourse "Hanshin Racecourse"). The race is also known as the **[Sankei](/wiki/Sankei_Shimbun "Sankei Shimbun") Ōsaka Hai** (産経大阪杯) (until 2016) and the Ōsaka Cup. The race is run in early April.

It was first run in 1957 over 1800 metres. The distance was increased to 1900 metres in 1966 before the race was run over its current distance for the first time in 1972.

Among the winners of the race have been [Katsuragi Ace](/wiki/Katsuragi_Ace "Katsuragi Ace"), [Tokai Teio](/wiki/Tokai_Teio "Tokai Teio"), [Mejiro McQueen](/wiki/Mejiro_McQueen "Mejiro McQueen"), [Air Groove](/wiki/Air_Groove "Air Groove"), [Neo Universe](/wiki/Neo_Universe "Neo Universe"), [Meisho Samson](/wiki/Meisho_Samson "Meisho Samson"), [Daiwa Scarlet](/wiki/Daiwa_Scarlet "Daiwa Scarlet"), [Orfevre](/wiki/Orfevre "Orfevre") and [Kizuna](/wiki/Kizuna_(horse) "Kizuna (horse)").

The Ōsaka Hai was promoted from Grade 2 status (as it has been since 1984) to Grade 1 in 2017.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Prior to 2017, the race was known as the Sankei Ōsaka Hai and served as a trial race for the spring edition of the [Tenno Sho](/wiki/Tenno_Sho "Tenno Sho").[[3]](#cite_note-3) From 2017 the winner will have a guaranteed and complimentary entry to that season's [Irish Champion Stakes](/wiki/Irish_Champion_Stakes "Irish Champion Stakes").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Records

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=%C5%8Csaka_Hai&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Records")]

**Speed record:**

* Fastest: Bellagio Opera (2025): 1:56.2
* Slowest: Tokai Teio (1992): 2:06.3

**Most wins by horse:**

* 2 — Sunrise Pegasus: (2002, 2005)
* 2 — Bellagio Opera: (2024, 2025)

**Most wins by jockey:**

* 6 — Yutaka Take: Mejiro McQueen (1993), Marvelous Sunday (1997), Air Groove (1998), Kizuna (2014), Kitasan Black (2017), Jack d'Or (2023)

**Most wins by trainer:**

* 3 — Yasutoshi Ikee: Dream Journey (2009), Orfevre (2013), Al Ain (2019)

**Most wins by owner:**

* 4 — Sunday Racing: Dream Journey (2009), Orfevre (2013), Al Ain (2019), Lucky Lilac (2020)
